titleStmt.title: Portland, OR, Steven Delamarter Collection, Delamarter Istanbul Codex Reproduction
Delamarter Istanbul Codex Reproduction

editionStmt.p: The initial version of this file was created from data kindly provided by the Ethiopic Manuscript Imaging Project directed by Steve Delamarter.

publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

licence.p: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

msContents.summary: Forty Nine Miracles of Jesus, ተአምረ፡ ኢየሱስ፡, Forty One Miracles of George, ተአምረ፡ ጊዮርጊስ፡, Image of the Trinity, መልክአ፡ ሥላሴ፡, Hymn, “Does not a man?”, Image of Mary’s Assumption, መልክአ፡ ፍልሰታ፡ ለማርያም፡

msItem.title: Taʾammǝra ʾIyasus
Short introduction, in a different hand
msItem.incipit: በስመ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ተአምሪሁ፡ እግዚእነ፡ ወአምላክነ፡ ወመድኃኒነ፡ [ኢ]የሱስ፡ ክርስቶስ፡ ሣህሉ፡ ወምሕረ[ቱ፡] ወስእለተ፡ እሙ፡ የሃሉ፡ ምስለ፡ ገብሩ፡ ክንፈ፡ ሩፋኤል፡ ለዓለመ፡ ዓለም፡ አሜ[ን]። ዝንቱ፡ ምሥጢር፡ አምላካዊ፡ ዘነገሮ፡ እግዚእነ፡ ወአምላክነ፡ ወመድኃኒ[ነ፡] ኢየሱስ፡ ክርስቶስ፡ ለረድኡ፡ ወ[ሐ]ዋርያሁ፡ ዮሐንስ፡ ወልደ፡ ዘብዴ[ዎ]ስ፡ እምቅድመ፡ ዕርገቱ ለእግዚ[እ]ነ፡ ወአምላክነ፡ ወመድኃኒነ፡ ኢየሱስ፡ [ክ]ርስቶስ፡ ውስተ፡ ሰማያት፡ (fol. 4r) በስመ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ንዌጥን፡ መጽሐፈ፡ ተአምሪሁ፡ ለእግዚእነ፡ ወአምላከነ፡ ወመድኃኒነ፡ ኢየሱስ፡ ክርስቶስ፡ ሣህሉ፡ ወምሕረቱ፡ የሀሉ፡ ምስለ፡ ገብሩ፡ ክንፈ፡ ሩፋኤል፡ ወምስለ

msItem.title: Introduction
The Nativity of Our Lord
Salome praises Our Lord
The throne of Satan was shaken by the birth of our Lord
Presentation of the Child Jesus to the Temple
The coming of the Magi and the flight to Egypt
The three robbers who met the Holy Family while returning from Egypt
The righteous man from Nazareth whose cattle were stolen
The fisherman whose fish were stolen
The stolen calf
Joseph and the lion
The flooded plantation
Jesus in the Temple with the Jews
The adulteress
The Samaritan women
Choosing of the Apostles
The raising from the dead the brother of a Jew and how animals praised Jesus
The healing of the man who was born blind
The raising from the dead of the son of the widow of Nain
Zacchaeus who could not pay his debts
Jesus at the tomb of Rachel
The watermelon plantation which was destroyed by insects
The hemorrhaging woman
The man from Ǝlḫәdr who was suffering from skin disease
The eradication of the locusts from Galilee and Judaea
The lions of Ascalon
Words of Jesus to his disciples at Jericho
Words of Jesus to his disciples at Mount Olive
Baptism and temptation of Jesus
Testimony of John the Evangelist
msItem.incipit: ።ክፍል። ይቤ፡ ዮሐንስ፡ ወንጌላዊ፡ አማን፡ እብለክሙ፡ ርኢክዎ፡ አነ፡ ለዮርዳኖስ፡ ገብአ፡ ድኅሬሁ፡ ጐዪዮ፡ አምቅድሜሁ፡ለእግዚእ፡ ኢየሱስ።

msItem.title: The wedding at Cana of Galilee
The raising of Lazarus from the dead
The words of our Lord on the future
msItem.incipit: Fol. 48rወወፂኦ፡ እግዚእነ፡ እምህየ፡ ምስለ፡ አርዳኢሁ፡ ሖረ፡ ወዓርገ፡ ደብረ፡ ሲና፡ ወኢየሩሳሌምሰ፡ መንገለ፡ ምዕራባ፡ ለደብረ፡ ሲና፡ ይእቲ።
Fol. 50vምዕራፍ። አእምሩ፡ ከመ፡ ኊላቌክሙ፡ ዕቀቡ፡ በኀበ፡ አቡየ፡ ወጽሑፍ፡ አስማቲክሙ፡ ውስተ፡ መጽሐፈ፡ ሕይወት። ወኢትወድቅ፡ አሐቲ፡ እምስእርተ፡ ርእስክሙ።
Fol. 53vምዕራፍ። አእምሩ፡ ኅልቀተ፡ መዋዕሊሁ፡ ለዝንቱ፡ ሕዝብ[፡] ይትመክዓብ፡ ላዕሌክሙ፡ እምኔሁ፡ ምንዳቤ። ወኢይፈቅዱ፡ይሠርውክሙ፡ እምገጸ፡ ምድር።
Fol. 58vክፍል። ወናሁ፡ አቅደምኩ፡ ወነገርኩክሙ፡ አኃውየ፡ ኵሎ፡ ዘይከውን፡ ዕቀቡ፡ ትእዛዝየ፡ ወጠይቁ። እስመ፡ ሀለወክሙ፡ ትሬእዩ፡ ኵሎ፡ ዘእቤለክሙ፡ እምግባረ፡ ዝንቱ፡ ዓለም፡ እስከ፡ ተፍጻሜት።
Fol. 60rክፍል፡ ናሁ፡ ነገርኩክሙ፡ ኵሎ፡ አኃውየ፡ ምሥጢራትየ፡ ወአማኀፀንኩክሙ፡ ከመ፡ ኢትግበሩ፡ ዘኢይደሉ፡ አንትሙሰ፡ ግበሩ፡ ትእዛዝየ፡ ወአምሩ፡ ከመ፡ የኃልፉ፡ ሰማያት፡ ወምድር።
Fol. 62rክፍል። ። ወእምዝ፡ ይቤሎሙ፡ እግዚእ፡ ኢየሱስ፡ ክርስቶስ፡ ለአርዳኢሁ። ብክዩ፡ አኃውየ፡ በእንተ፡ ኢየሩሳሌም፡ ዘይረክባ፡ በደኃሪ፡ መዋዕል። እስመ፡ እንስሳ፡ በድው፡ ወአዕዱገ፡ ገዳም፡ ይከይድዋ።

msItem.title: Palm Sunday
The woman who anointed Jesus
The Last Supper, the washing of the feet and the arrest of Jesus
From Passion to burial
msItem.note: in two parts with the second part starting on fol. 76v

msItem.title: The end of Judas Iscariot
Resurrection of Jesus
The women at the tomb
msItem.incipit: ወበእሑድ፡ ሰንበት ጊዜ፡ ጽባሕ፡ እንዘ፡ ዓዲሁ፡ ጽልመት፡ መጽአ፡ አንስት፡ ኀበ፡ መቃብር፡ ወምስሌሆን። መዓጥን፡ ወአፈው፡ ከመ፡ ይቅብዓሁ፡ ለሥጋ፡ እግዚእነ፡ በከመ፡ ሕገ፡ ደቂቅ፡ እስራኤል፡

msItem.title: The appearance of Jesus to Cleopas and Nicodemus
Jesus prevents Cleopas, Nicodemus et al from attacking the Jews in revenge for killing him
msItem.incipit: ክፍል። ወእምዝ፡ ሖሩ፡ ኢየሩሳሌም፡ ኀበ፡ ፲ወ፪አርዳእ፡ ወነገሮሙ፡1 ኵሎ፡ ዘኮነ። ወዓዲ[፡] ዜነውዎሙ፡ ዘይቤሎሙ፡ መልአክ። ወእምዝ፡ አስተጋብኡ፡ ኒቆዲሞስ፡ ወቀለዮጳ፡ ወገማልያል፡ ወናትናኤል፡ መጠነ፡ ፭፻፡ ብእሲ፡ እምእለ፡ አምኑ፡ በእግዚእነ፡ ኢየሱስ፡ ክርስቶስ፡ ወምስሌሆሙ፡ ንዋየ፡ ሐቅል

msItem.title: The conspiracy of the Jews to arrest Joseph of Armathea
The appearance of Jesus to the Apostles at the Sea of Tiberias
The appearance of Jesus to the Apostles in the Cenacle
The Ascension of Jesus
Pentecost
The Apostles go out to preach the New Testament
Taʾammǝra Giyorgis
msItem.incipit: ንዌጥን፡ በረድኤተ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ ጽሒፈ፡ ተአምራት፡ ዘገብረ፡ ሎቱ፡ ለሰማዕተ ልዳ፡ መክብበ፡ ሰማዕታት፡ ቅዱስ፡ ወብፁዕ፡ መስተጋድል፡ ማር፡ ጊዮርጊስ፡ ዘመጠጠ፡ ነፍሶ፡ ለሞት፡ በእንተ፡ ክርስቶስ፡ አምጻኤ፡ ዓለማት፡ እምኀበ፡ ኢከዊን፡ ኀበ፡ ኵነት። በስመ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡። ወእምድኅረ፡ ተፈጸመ፡ ስምዑ፡ አምጽኡ፡ ሥጋሁ፡ ውስተ፡ ልዳ፡ ሀገሩ። ወሐነጹ፡ ቤተ፡ ክርስቲያን፡ ወአንበሩ፡ ሥጋሁ፡ ውስቴታ።

msItem.title: The miracle Our Lord performed for his martyr
Ǝndәrәyas and his wife who wanted to build a church in the name of the martyr
The healing a man with an evil spirit at the consecration of the church
The Jew who stole church property
Naṭolis, the son of Niqoros from Persia
The two brothers who met a lion while traveling
Säqrqäṭәs, the lame rich Christian from Jerusalem who vowed to give money to the church if he could walk by the help of the martyr
The man who stole from the property of the church
Awhәyos, the wicked ruler of Egypt
Awlogyos from Antioch
The head of the church that is reluctant to pay for the servants of the church
How the martyr promised to help the man who was assigned by Emperor Constantine to build a church in the name of the martyr
Bifan from Bilaqon and his wife
How Diocletian was angry about the miracles performed in the church of St. George at Lydda
The martyr appears riding his while horse on the river Nile
How Christians and Muslims visited the church of the martyr hearing his miracles worked there
How the martyr helped a Muslim who was lost in the desert
The Muslim who did not keep his vow to give oil to the church
How the relics of the martyr raised George from the dead
The Muslim in Bәba whom the Jews accused because he celebrated the martyr
The Muslim official in Bәba who hated St. George and the Christians
The miracle in the church of St. George at Mu‘allaqa, called Dor al-Tiqa
The Muslim woman who took communion with the Christians but later took it out of her mouth and kept it in a piece of cloth
How St. George opened the door for the service of matrimony
How St. George guarded the church the custodian forgot to close
Abba Samu’el of Qalamun and the angel who could not fly
The conspiracy of the Muslims to take over the church of St. George and the martyr from the Christians
The ruler who was reluctant to give permission for the renewal of the church of St. George
How St. George saved the house of a monk from fire
How St. George helped a man against a host of Muslim who came to convert the Christians to their faith
How St. George helped the custodian who barred a serpent from entering the church of the martyr
How St. George helped a boy to kill a serpent that was to attack him
The beer container that did not break when it fell in a precipice
The monk from whom the martyr took away his icon
The king who moved into the new church built for St. George
The fleet that could not move because the captain did not give his vow to the church
The fisherman whom the martyr and Mähәmnan helped in pulling his net overfilled with fish
msItem.incipit: ተአምሪሁ፡ ወሀሎ፡ ፩ብእሲ፡ መሠግረ፡ ዓሣ፡ ወወደየ፡ መርበብቶ፡ ውስተ፡ ባሕር፡ ወተእህዘ፡ ዓሣ፡ ብዙኅ፡ ወሶበ፡ ሰሐበ፡ ተበትከ፡ መሥገርቱ፡ ወከልሐ፡ እንዘ፡ ይብል[፡] ቅዱስ፡ መርህምናም፡ ርድአኒ። ወበጊዜሃ፡ መጽኡ፡ ቅዱስ፡ ጊዮርጊስ፡ ወቅዱስ፡ መርምህናም፡

msItem.title: The witness of a righteous monk that St. George is the greatest of all saints
How eggs falling from the scaffolding of the painter did not break
How painter who fell from his scaffolding was not hurt
How a new church was built in place of another new church that the martyr did not like
Malkǝʾa Śǝllāse
msItem.incipit: ሰላም፡ እብል፡ ለዘኮነ፡ ነግደ። ውስተ፡ ማኅደር፡ ይርፍቅ፡ ኀበ፡ ይበልዕ፡ ማዕደ። ሥላሴሁ፡ አምላክ፡ ከመ፡ እምሰማያት፡ ወረደ። ፀገወ፡ ዮም፡ ከመ፡ ውእቱ፡ ፈቀደ። ለአብርሃም፡ ወልደ፡ ወለሰዶም፡ ነደ፡
msItem.note: concluded with a short greeting to the Trinity

msItem.title: The collection of hymns, “Does not a man?” አካኑ፡ ብእሲ፡
msItem.note: Composed to conclude each of the original thirty-two miracles of Mary

msItem.title: Malkǝʾa fǝlsatā
An Account of Fallen Angels
msItem.note: incomplete at the beginning and the end. An account of how the angel Säbsanǝyos and his friends claimed they were God; how they fell from heaven and how Adam replaced them
msItem.incipit: ወሶበ፡ አእመረ፡ ጥዩቀ፡ ውእቱ፡ ርጉም፡ ከመ፡ ውእቱ፡ ፍጡር፡ እመንፈስ፡ ወብርሃን፡ ቀዳማዊ፡ ቀደመ፡ በህሊናሁ፡ ወይቤ፡ በነፍሱ፡ ለምንት፡ እሬሲ፡ ክብርየ፡ ለባዕድ፡ ወለምንት፡ እሴብህ፡ ወለምንት፡ ኢይሴባሕ፡ አነ፡ ወተናገሮሙ፡ ሰብሳንዮስ፡ ለመላእክት፡ እለ፡ ምስሌሁ:

collation.note: Quire descriptions: quires 1–3, 6–18 balanced; quires 4–5 unbalanced.

handNote.desc: The words Mary and George are rubricated

decoNote.desc: Virgin and Child in a crude hand

item.desc: The first stanza of the hymn “Does not a man?” አካኑ፡ ብእሲ፡.

Note of ownership by Däjazmač Kǝnfu. Text: ለዝንቱ፡ መጽሐፍ፡ ዘአጥረዮ፡ በንዋዩ፡ ደጅ፡ አዝማች፡ ክንፉ፡ ዘወሀባ፡ ለ(??) ማርያም፡ እግዝእተ፡ ኲሉ፡ ዓለም፡ ከመ፡ ይኩኖ፡ ለመድኃኒተ፡ ሥጋ፡ ወነፍስ፡ ለዝ፡ ተአምረ፡ ኢየሱስ፡ ወተአምረ፡ ጊዮርጊስ፡ ዘሠረቆ፡ ወዘፈሐቆ፡ በሥልጣነ፡ ጴጥሮስ፡ ወጳውሎስ፡ ውጉዘ፡ ለይኩን። Translation: Däjazmač Kǝnfu is the one who bought this book with his wealth and gave it to the Church [??] Maryam, Lady of the Whole World, in order to be for the salvation of body and soul. Anyone who steals it and defaces this Miracles of Jesus and Miracles of George shall be excommunicated by the authority of Peter and Paul.
Copied for Wäldä Kiros (systematically erased and replaced by Kǝnfu/ Kǝnfä Rufa’el)
(etc.) words of text are written interlinearly;
lines of text are written interlinearly;
lines of text are written in the upper margin with a symbol (┬) marking the location where the text is to be inserted (140r), and in another case where the symbol (+) is used (f. );

binding.decoNote: This book, clearly produced in the late 20th or early 21st century, is a photographic reproduction of an Ethiopian codex. Its own measurements are 267 x 207 x 24 mm. Judging by the appearance of the letters, it may be that the reproduction is at or near a 1:1 ratio, however there is no way to be sure of this. The reproduction is bound as a western, hard-cover book with head and tail bands and end sheets. The end sheet pasted on the inside of the front cover is a photograph of the inside cover of the original codex showing leather turn-ins and a linen covering in between the turn-ins. The same is true of the inside of the back cover. On the front and back of the book are pasted photographs of the original cover of the leather-bound codex, though they appear to be turned around, i.e., the images in the tooled leather are upside down in relation to the body of the codex and it seems likely that the image of the cross was on the front (not the back) and the image of the church was on the back (not the front).

As a photographic reproduction, only some of the features of the original codex can be reconstructed. Parchment, four Coptic chain stitches attached with bridle attachments to boards covered with tooled leather over decorative linen patches visible inside the turn-ins on front and back cover, perhaps rebound (see the excess string between fols. 148, 149).

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)

t:revisionDesc.change: Created XML record from EMIP Collection Metadata.xsls
Added columns/lines, binding desc, dating info.
